nodes.name: CatalogGeneration
nodes.owns: the one catalog-meaning freshness clock; sole door that mints projection Generation stamps from a proven counter
forbids: bare u64 freshness
public Generation::new(raw)

nodes.name: Domain
nodes.owns: arena+epoch+visibility admission; a raw u32 code is meaningful only under a proven Domain; cross-Domain code comparison is invalid
forbids: fabricating a Domain to bless arbitrary codes
comparing or joining codes across differing arena or epoch
converts: Domain -> ExecRows (Rows::admit(observer) -> AdmittedRows, under this Domain)
nodes.gate: join_project / admit refuse cross-arena/epoch typed; raw-code use requires admission (value-plane.md)

nodes.name: ExecDedup
nodes.owns: the fixpoint dedup identity: packed admitted code tuples, u32-slice equality, no canonical encode in the hot loop
forbids: inserting codes that did not come from an admitted ExecRows
mixing domains in one sink
bare `&[u32]` insert
converts: ExecDedup -> ExecRows (to_exec: the distinct rows, same Domain)

nodes.name: ExecRows
nodes.owns: the admitted execution currency; codes are unforgeable (private field, no raw-code door)
forbids: from_raw over raw codes
injecting codes into Rows
reconstruction outside admission
converts: ExecRows -> StorageKey (canonical bytes only at a storage/output boundary, never durable identity)
nodes.gate: no raw-code door exposed; zero-canonical-encode-in-fixpoint law (#120)

nodes.name: IndexGeneration
nodes.owns: per-index monotone rebuild counter that witnesses catalog freshness for one index
forbids: bare integer index freshness outside this authority
converts: IndexGeneration -> CatalogGeneration (lift into the catalog clock)
nodes.gate: index rebuild stamps only through IndexGeneration::witness (#337)

nodes.name: QueryDomainAdmission
nodes.owns: admission of engine search hits into the query execution currency as codes; decoded engine tuples never cross the SearchRA seam
forbids: holding decoded engine Tuple vectors as the SearchRA hit currency
nodes.gate: SearchRA hit currency is QueryDomainAdmission codes only (#337)

nodes.name: RelationGeneration
nodes.owns: per-relation monotone write counter that witnesses catalog freshness for one relation
forbids: bare AtomicU64 load standing for relation freshness without this witness
converts: RelationGeneration -> CatalogGeneration (lift into the catalog clock)
nodes.gate: segment freshness samples only through RelationGeneration::witness (#337)

nodes.name: ResidentIndexKey
nodes.owns: rebuildable projection residency identity — a sealed kind's cache key under one Generation
forbids: bare RelationId standing for resident index identity across engines
nodes.gate: segment/index resident maps keyed only by ResidentIndexKey (#337)

nodes.name: StorageKey
nodes.owns: canonical storage identity in the memcmp keyspace; bytewise order equals DataValue structural order (byte-order law)
forbids: leaking codes out of a StorageKey
forging bytes bypassing canonical encode
confusing storage identity with record/entity identity
treating a StorageKey as a bare TupleKey
converts: StorageKey -> Domain (admitted into an arena via push_encoded)

nodes.name: Tuple
nodes.owns: row identity as an ordered `DataValue` sequence, distinct from any bare `Vec<DataValue>`
forbids: a bare `Vec<DataValue>` standing in for row authority (no Deref/DerefMut/From)

nodes.name: TupleKey
nodes.owns: bare-tuple memcmp identity (no relation prefix); bytewise order equals DataValue structural order (byte-order law)
forbids: forging bytes bypassing canonical encode
treating a TupleKey as a relation-prefixed StorageKey
converts: TupleKey -> StorageKey (prefix with RelationId at the storage boundary)

edges.context: canonical bytes only at a storage/output boundary, never durable identity
to_exec: the distinct rows, same Domain
prefix with RelationId at the storage boundary
admitted into an arena via push_encoded

findings.note: Report.downcast_ref recovering typed errors in product code
Report.downcast_ref recovering typed errors in product code
Deref dissolves the TupleKey authority boundary
findings.allowlisted: read-only &[u8] view: the one legal TupleKey -> stored-bytes conversion; field private, no DerefMut, every mint validates — bytes can be read, never forged

findings.note: Deref dissolves the StorageKey authority boundary
findings.allowlisted: read-only &[u8] view: the one legal StorageKey -> stored-bytes conversion; field private, no DerefMut, every mint validates — bytes can be read, never forged
named corruption/bypass TEST asserting that a forged stamp refuses typed — it proves the door is shut, it is not a door
total boundary constructor: every i64 is a valid validity-timestamp micros value; parse-at-boundary, not an authority bypass
